src/main/java/com/aerotravel/flightticke.general_idea: Change the ID generation strategy from AUTO to IDENTITY to ensure compatibility with specific database drivers or JPA provider behaviors in the new environment.
src/main/java/com/aerotravel/flightticke.why_exists: GenerationType.AUTO may default to TABLE or SEQUENCE in some JPA providers, which might not be supported or desired by the target database configuration; IDENTITY is explicit for auto-increment columns.

src/main/java/com/aerotravel/flightticke.general_idea: Change the ID field type from Integer to Long to support larger primary key values.
src/main/java/com/aerotravel/flightticke.why_exists: Integer has a smaller range than Long; if the database column is BIGINT or if the application expects Long IDs, using Integer causes compilation errors or runtime overflow issues.

src/main/java/com/aerotravel/flightticke.general_idea: Initialize the roles list with an empty ArrayList to prevent NullPointerExceptions when accessing roles on a newly built User object.
src/main/java/com/aerotravel/flightticke.why_exists: Without initialization, the roles field is null by default, leading to NullPointerExceptions if code attempts to add roles or iterate over them without null checks.

src/main/java/com/aerotravel/flightticke.general_idea: Add explicit EAGER fetching for the roles relationship to ensure roles are loaded immediately with the user.
src/main/java/com/aerotravel/flightticke.why_exists: Default fetch type for ManyToMany is LAZY; changing to EAGER prevents LazyInitializationExceptions in contexts where the persistence context is closed, though this is often a design choice rather than a strict JDK migration requirement.
